title: Minimum Absolute Difference in BST
hints: In a BST, the minimum difference between any two nodes must occur between adjacent values in the sorted order of all node values.
Perform an in-order traversal to visit nodes in ascending order, allowing you to compare each node only with its immediate predecessor.
The minimum difference is simply the smallest gap between consecutive values encountered during the traversal; initialize the minimum with a large value and update it as you go.
